sections.paragraph:
ἐξαρτάω pf. pass. ptc. ἐξηρτημένος (ἀρτάω ‘fasten to, hang’; Eur. et al.; Polyb. 18, 1, 4; Hero Alex. I p. 434, 16; PTebt 769, 1 [III b.c.]).

① to cause to be suspended by attachment to someth., hang up τινός by someth. (Proclus on Pla., Cratyl. p. 89, 11 Pasqu.) τῶν πλοκάμων by the braids of their hair ApcPt 9:24.

② pass. (Eur., X. et al.; POxy 471, 83 [II a.d.]; Ex 28:7) to be closely associated with someone, be attached to, be an adherent of, τινός someone (Eur., Suppl. 735; Plut., Galb. 1056 [8, 2], Arat. 1047 [42, 1], Gracchi 837 [27, 4] ἐξηρτημένον αὐτοῦ πλῆθος) Mk 3:21 v.l.—DELG s.v. 2 ἀείρω.

  1. pf. = perfect

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

ptc. ptc. = participle

Eur Eur , V b.c.—List 5

et al. et al. = et alii (and others)

Polyb Polyb , III–II b.c.—List 5

Hero Alex. Hero Alex. = Hero(n) of Alexandria, I? b.c./I a.d.—List 5

PTebt PTebt = The Tebtunis Papyri—List 4

someth. someth. = something

Pla Pla , V–IV b.c.; s. also Ps.-Plato—List 5

ApcPt ApcPt = Apocalypse of Peter—List 1

  1. X. = Xenophon, V–IV b.c.—List 5

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Suppl.

Suppl. = Supplement (to serials)

suppl. = supplement, supplied (in grammatical references)

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Arat Arat of Soli in Cilicia, IV–III b.c.—List 5

v.l. v.l. = varia lectio (variant reading)

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 346
